reason: Moses is portrayed as a regular person with an explicit name (מֹשֶׁה, Moses), a defined profession (shepherd of Jethro's flock as stated in the opening verse), and extensive dialogue throughout the passage. He speaks multiple times, including his statement about turning aside to see the burning bush, his response to God's call (הִנֵּֽנִי, 'Here I am'), his question to God (מִ֣י אָנֹ֔כִי כִּ֥י אֵלֵ֖ךְ אֶל־ פַּרְעֹ֑ה, 'Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh?'), and his concerns about how to address the Israelites. Additionally, Jethro is mentioned by name with the profession of 'priest of Midian,' though without direct speech in this passage.
